Q1 | Mechanics Easydiagram
ok conf:1.00
From a disc of radius R and mass M, a circular hole of diameter R, whose rim passes through the centre is cut. What is the moment of inertia of the remaining part of the disc about a perpendicular axis, passing through the centre?
(1) 15 MR^2/32
(2) 13 MR^2/32 ✓ Correct
(3) 11 MR^2/32
(4) 9 MR^2/32
Solution: Moment of inertia of the complete disc about perpendicular axis passing through center is I_disc = MR^2/2. Mass of cut part = M' = (M/(πR²)) * π(R/2)² = M/4. Moment of inertia of cut part about its own center = I_hole_self = M'(R/2)²/2 = (M/4)(R/2)²/2 = MR²/32. Distance of center of hole from center of disc = R/2. By parallel axis theorem, Moment of inertia of cut part about center of disc = I_hole_disc = I_hole_self + M'(R/2)² = MR²/32 + (M/4)(R/2)² = MR²/32 + MR²/16 = (MR² + 2MR²)/32 = 3MR²/32. Moment of inertia of remaining part = I_disc - I_hole_disc = MR²/2 - 3MR²/32 = (16MR² - 3MR²)/32 =...
[solution diagram]

Q2 | Electromagnetism Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A square loop ABCD carrying a current i, is placed near and coplanar with a long straight conductor XY carrying a current I, the net force on the loop will be: [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) 2µ₀Ii/(3π) ✓ Correct
(2) µ₀Ii/(2π)
(3) 2µ₀IiL/(3π)
(4) µ₀IiL/(2π)
Solution: Force on side AD (at distance L/2) = F₁ = (µ₀IiL)/(2π(L/2)) = (µ₀Ii)/(π). Force on side BC (at distance 3L/2) = F₂ = (µ₀IiL)/(2π(3L/2)) = (µ₀Ii)/(3π). The forces on AB and CD are equal and opposite, so they cancel. The net force F_net = F₁ - F₂ = (µ₀Ii)/(π) - (µ₀Ii)/(3π) = (2µ₀Ii)/(3π). [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q3 | Electromagnetism Medium
ok conf:1.00
The magnetic susceptibility is negative for:
(1) diamagnetic material only ✓ Correct
(2) paramagnetic material only
(3) ferromagnetic material only
(4) paramagnetic and ferromagnetic materials
Solution: Magnetic susceptibility χm is negative for diamagnetic substance only.
Q4 | Waves Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A siren emitting a sound of frequency 800 Hz moves away from an observer towards a cliff at a speed of 15 ms⁻¹. Then, the frequency of sound that the observer hears in the echo reflected from the cliff is: (Take velocity of sound in air = 330 ms⁻¹) [DIAGRAM]
(1) 765 Hz
(2) 800 Hz
(3) 838 Hz ✓ Correct
(4) 885 Hz
Solution: Frequency of siren f₀ = 800 Hz. Speed of source V_source = 15 m/s. Speed of sound in air v = 330 m/s. The observer hears the echo reflected from the cliff. First, consider the cliff as an observer. The siren moves towards the cliff. The apparent frequency heard by the cliff (f_cliff) = f₀ * (v / (v - V_source)) = 800 * (330 / (330 - 15)) = 800 * (330 / 315). Now, the cliff acts as a source emitting sound at frequency f_cliff, and the observer is stationary while the cliff is also stationary. However, the problem description implies the source (siren) is moving away from the observer TOWARDS a ...
[solution diagram]

Q5 | Electromagnetism Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A capacitor of 2µF is charged as shown in the diagram. When the switch S is turned to position 2, the percentage of its stored energy dissipated is: [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) 0%
(2) 20%
(3) 75%
(4) 80% ✓ Correct
Solution: Initially, the 2µF capacitor is charged to voltage V. Its charge Q = CV = 2µF * V. Stored energy U₁ = (1/2)CV² = (1/2)(2µF)V² = V². When the switch is moved to position 2, the 2µF capacitor is connected in parallel with an 8µF capacitor. The total charge remains conserved (Q_total = 2V). The new equivalent capacitance C_eq = 2µF + 8µF = 10µF. The common voltage across them V' = Q_total / C_eq = (2V) / (10µF) = V/5. The final stored energy U₂ = (1/2)C_eq(V')² = (1/2)(10µF)(V/5)² = (1/2)(10µF)(V²/25) = (1/2)(2/5)µFV² = (1/5)µFV² = V²/5. Energy dissipated = U₁ - U₂ = V² - V²/5 = 4V²/5. Percentage...
[solution diagram]


Q6 | Optics Hard
ok conf:1.00
In a diffraction pattern due to a single slit of width ‘a', the first minimum is observed at an angle 30°when light of wavelength 5000 Å is incident on the slit. The first secondary maximum is observed at an angle of:
(1) sin⁻¹(1/4)
(2) sin⁻¹(2/3)
(3) sin⁻¹(1/2)
(4) sin⁻¹(3/4) ✓ Correct
Solution: For first minimum in single slit diffraction, a sinθ = mλ. Here m=1, θ=30°. So, a sin(30°) = λ => a(1/2) = λ => a = 2λ. For first secondary maximum, a sinθ' = (2m+1)λ/2. Here m=1. So, a sinθ' = (2(1)+1)λ/2 = 3λ/2. Substitute a = 2λ: (2λ) sinθ' = 3λ/2 => sinθ' = 3/4. Therefore, θ' = sin⁻¹(3/4).
Q7 | Gravitation Hard
ok conf:1.00
At what height from the surface of earth the gravitation potential and the value of g are -5.4 × 10⁷J kg⁻² and 6.0 ms⁻² respectively? Take the radius of earth as 6400 km:
(1) 2600 km ✓ Correct
(2) 1600 km
(3) 1400 km
(4) 2000 km
Solution: Gravitational potential V = -GM/(R+h) = -5.4 × 10⁷ J/kg. Acceleration due to gravity g' = GM/(R+h)² = 6.0 m/s². Dividing V by g': V/g' = (-GM/(R+h)) / (GM/(R+h)²) = -(R+h). So, -(R+h) = (-5.4 × 10⁷) / 6.0 = -9 × 10⁶ m. R+h = 9 × 10⁶ m = 9000 km. Given R = 6400 km. h = 9000 km - 6400 km = 2600 km.
Q8 | Electromagnetism Medium
ok conf:1.00
Out of the following options which one can be used to produce a propagating electromagnetic wave?
(1) A charge moving at constant velocity
(2) A stationary charge
(3) A chargeless particle
(4) An accelerating charge ✓ Correct
Solution: An accelerating charge can produce electromagnetic wave.
Q9 | Electrostatics Harddiagram
ok conf:1.00
Two identical charged spheres suspended from a common point by two massless strings of lengths l, are initially at a distance d (d << l) apart because of their mutual repulsion. The charges begin to leak from both the spheres at a constant rate. As a result, the spheres approach each other with a velocity u. Then u varies as a function of the distance x between the spheres, as [DIAGRAM]
(1) v ∝ x^(1/2)
(2) v ∝ x
(3) v ∝ x^(-1/2) ✓ Correct
(4) v ∝ x^(-1)
Solution: From the equilibrium of forces (tension T, weight mg, electrostatic force F_e): F_e = mg tanθ. Since θ is small, tanθ ≈ sinθ ≈ x/(2l). So, (kq²)/x² = mg (x/(2l)). This gives q² = (mg x³)/(2kl), where k is Coulomb's constant. So, q ∝ x^(3/2). Given that the charges leak at a constant rate, dq/dt = constant. Differentiating q = C x^(3/2) with respect to time: dq/dt = C * (3/2) x^(1/2) (dx/dt). Since dq/dt is constant, C * (3/2) x^(1/2) v = constant (where v = dx/dt). Therefore, v ∝ x^(-1/2). [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q10 | Waves Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A uniform rope of length L and mass m₁, hangs vertically from a rigid support. A block of mass m₂ is attached to the free end of the rope. A transverse pulse of wavelength λ₁ is produced at the lower end of the rope. The wavelength of the pulse when it reaches the top of the rope is λ₂. The ratio λ₂/λ₁ is: [DIAGRAM]
(1) √(m₁/m₂)
(2) √((m₁+m₂)/m₂) ✓ Correct
(3) √(m₂/(m₁+m₂))
(4) √(m₁/(m₁+m₂))
Solution: The speed of a transverse wave in a string is given by v = √(T/μ), where T is the tension and μ is the linear mass density. The wavelength λ = v/f. Since the frequency (f) of the pulse remains constant, λ ∝ v ∝ √T. At the lower end of the rope (where m₂ is attached), the tension T₁ = m₂g. So, λ₁ ∝ √(m₂g). At the top end of the rope, the tension T₂ = (m₁ + m₂)g (due to the mass of the rope m₁ and the block m₂). So, λ₂ ∝ √((m₁ + m₂)g). The ratio λ₂/λ₁ = √(T₂/T₁) = √(((m₁ + m₂)g) / (m₂g)) = √((m₁ + m₂)/m₂). [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q11 | Thermal physics Easydiagram
ok conf:1.00
A refrigerator works between 4°C and 30°C. It is required to remove 600 calories of heat every second in order to keep the temperature of the refrigerated space constant. The power required is: (Take 1 cal = 4.2 Joules) [DIAGRAM]
(1) 2.365 W
(2) 23.65 W
(3) 236.5 W ✓ Correct
(4) 2365 W
Solution: Lower temperature T_L = 4°C = 277 K. Higher temperature T_H = 30°C = 303 K. Heat removed per second Q_L = 600 cal/s. Coefficient of performance of a refrigerator COP = Q_L/W = T_L/(T_H - T_L), where W is the work done per second (power). So, W = Q_L * (T_H - T_L) / T_L. W = 600 cal/s * (303 - 277) / 277 = 600 * 26 / 277 cal/s. Convert to Watts (J/s): W = (600 * 26 / 277) * 4.2 J/s = 236.5 W. [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q12 | Waves Medium
ok conf:1.00
An air column, closed at one end and open at the other, resonates with a tuning fork when the smallest length of the column is 50 cm. The next larger length of the column resonating with the same tuning fork is:
(1) 66.7 cm
(2) 100 cm
(3) 150cm ✓ Correct
(4) 200 cm
Solution: For a closed organ pipe (one end closed, one end open), the resonant lengths are given by L = (2n-1)λ/4, where n=1, 2, 3... The smallest length (n=1) is L₁ = λ/4 = 50 cm. So, the wavelength λ = 4 * 50 cm = 200 cm. The next larger length corresponds to n=2, which is the third harmonic: L₂ = (2(2)-1)λ/4 = 3λ/4. L₂ = 3 * (200 cm) / 4 = 150 cm.
Q13 | Modern physics Harddiagram
ok conf:1.00
Consider the junction diode as ideal. The value of current flowing through AB is: [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) 0 A
(2) 10⁻² A ✓ Correct
(3) 10⁻¹ A
(4) 10⁻³ A
Solution: The potential at point A is +4 V and at point B is -6 V. Since the P-side is at a higher potential (+4 V) than the N-side (-6 V), the diode is forward biased. For an ideal diode, it behaves as a short circuit in forward bias. So, the voltage drop across the diode is 0 V. The voltage across the 1 kΩ resistor is V_R = V_A - V_B = 4 V - (-6 V) = 10 V. The current I = V_R / R = 10 V / 1 kΩ = 10 V / 1000 Ω = 0.01 A = 10⁻² A.
Q14 | Electrostatics Medium
ok conf:1.00
The charge flowing through a resistance R varies with time t as Q = at - bt² where a and b are positive constants. The total heat produced in R is:
(1) a³R/(6b) ✓ Correct
(2) a³R/(3b)
(3) a³R/(2b)
(4) a³R/b
Solution: Current i = dQ/dt = d(at - bt²)/dt = a - 2bt. Heat produced in resistance R is H = ∫i²R dt. The current flows until it becomes zero, so a - 2bt = 0 => t = a/(2b). We integrate from t=0 to t=a/(2b). H = ∫₀^(a/2b) (a - 2bt)² R dt = R ∫₀^(a/2b) (a - 2bt)² dt. Let u = a - 2bt, then du = -2b dt. When t=0, u=a. When t=a/(2b), u=0. H = R ∫_a^0 u² (-du/(2b)) = (R/(-2b)) [u³/3]_a^0 = (-R/(6b)) (0³ - a³) = (-R/(6b))(-a³) = a³R/(6b).
Q15 | Thermal physics Hard
ok conf:1.00
A black body is at a temperature of 5760 K. The energy of radiation emitted by the body at wavelength 250 nm is U₁, at wavelength 500 nm is U₂ and that at 1000 nm is U₃. Wien's constant, b = 2.88 × 10⁶ nmK. Which of the following is correct?
(1) U₁ = 0
(2) U₃ = 0
(3) U₁ > U₂ ✓ Correct
(4) U₂ > U₁
Solution: According to Wien's displacement law, λ_max T = b. λ_max = b/T = (2.88 × 10⁶ nmK) / 5760 K = 500 nm. The intensity of radiation is maximum at λ_max = 500 nm, which corresponds to U₂. So, U₂ should be the maximum intensity. Thus, U₂ > U₁ and U₂ > U₃. The solution provided in the image states `u₁ > u₂`, which contradicts the principle that intensity is maximum at λ_max. Assuming there might be an error in the given solution's derivation or option choice, or it relates to a specific feature of the curve which is not universally taught as `U₁ > U₂` when `U₂` is at peak. However, following the prov...
Q16 | Solid and fluid mecahnics Medium
ok conf:1.00
Coefficient of linear expansion of brass and steel rods are α₁ and α₂. Lengths of brass and steel rods are l₁ and l₂ respectively. If (l₂ - l₁) is maintained same at all temperatures, which one of the following relations holds good?
(1) α₁l₂ = α₂l₁
(2) α₁l₁² = α₂l₂²
(3) α₁l₂² = α₂l₁²
(4) α₁l₁ = α₂l₂ ✓ Correct
Solution: The change in length ΔL for a linear expansion is given by ΔL = L₀αΔT. If the difference in lengths (l₂ - l₁) is maintained same at all temperatures, it means the change in difference is zero. So, Δ(l₂ - l₁) = 0. This implies Δl₂ = Δl₁. Therefore, l₂α₂ΔT = l₁α₁ΔT. Since ΔT is common, l₂α₂ = l₁α₁.
Q17 | Modern physics Medium
ok conf:1.00
A npn transistor is connected in common emitter configuration in a given amplifier. A load resistance of 800 Ω is connected in the collector circuit and the voltage drop across it is 0.8 V. If the current amplification factor is 0.96 and the input resistance of the circuit is 192 Ω, the voltage gain and the power gain of the amplifier will respectively be:
(1) 4, 3.84 ✓ Correct
(2) 3.69, 3.84
(3) 4, 4
(4) 4, 3.69
Solution: Load resistance R_C = 800 Ω. Voltage drop across R_C = V_C = 0.8 V. Current amplification factor β = 0.96. Input resistance R_B = 192 Ω. Collector current I_C = V_C/R_C = 0.8 V / 800 Ω = 0.001 A. Base current I_B = I_C/β = 0.001 A / 0.96. Input voltage V_B = I_B * R_B = (0.001 / 0.96) * 192 = 0.2 V. Voltage gain A_V = V_C / V_B = 0.8 V / 0.2 V = 4. Power gain A_P = β * A_V = 0.96 * 4 = 3.84.
Q18 | Optics Medium
ok conf:1.00
The intensity at the maximum in a Young's double slit experiment is I₀. Distance between two slits is d = 5λ, where λ is the wavelength of light used in the experiment. What will be the intensity in front of one of the slits on the screen placed at a distance D = 10 d?
(1) I₀
(2) I₀/4
(3) I₀/2
(4) 3I₀/4 ✓ Correct
Solution: In YDSE, maximum intensity I_max = I₀. The path difference (Δx) at a point y from the center is Δx = yd/D. In front of one of the slits, y = d/2. So, Δx = (d/2) * (d/D) = d²/(2D). Given d = 5λ and D = 10d = 10(5λ) = 50λ. Substitute these values: Δx = (5λ)² / (2 * 50λ) = 25λ² / (100λ) = λ/4. The corresponding phase difference (φ) is φ = (2π/λ) * Δx = (2π/λ) * (λ/4) = π/2. The intensity I = I_max cos²(φ/2) = I₀ cos²((π/2)/2) = I₀ cos²(π/4) = I₀ * (1/√2)² = I₀/2. (Note: There is a discrepancy between the calculated result I₀/2 and the provided correct answer 3I₀/4. Following the provided solution...
Q19 | Mechanics Easy
ok conf:1.00
A uniform circular disc of radius 50 cm at rest is free to turn about an axis which is perpendicular to its plane and passes through its centre. It is subjected to a torque which produces a constant angular acceleration of 2.0 rad s⁻². Its net acceleration in ms⁻² at the end of 2.0 s is approximately:
(1) 8.0 ✓ Correct
(2) 7.0
(3) 6.0
(4) 3.0
Solution: Radius r = 50 cm = 0.5 m. Angular acceleration α = 2.0 rad/s². Initial angular velocity ω₀ = 0 (at rest). At t = 2.0 s, angular velocity ω = ω₀ + αt = 0 + (2.0 rad/s²)(2.0 s) = 4.0 rad/s. Tangential acceleration a_t = rα = (0.5 m)(2.0 rad/s²) = 1.0 m/s². Centripetal acceleration a_c = rω² = (0.5 m)(4.0 rad/s)² = 0.5 * 16 m/s² = 8.0 m/s². Net acceleration a_net = √(a_t² + a_c²) = √((1.0)² + (8.0)²) = √(1 + 64) = √65 ≈ 8.06 m/s². Approximately 8.0 m/s².
Q20 | Dual nature of radiation and matter Easy
ok conf:1.00
An electron of mass m and a photon have same energy E. the ratio of de-Broglie wavelengths associated with them is: (c being velocity of light)
(1) (1/c) * √(E/(2m)) ✓ Correct
(2) (1/c) * √(2m/E)
(3) c * √(2mE)
(4) (1/c) * √(2mE)
Solution: For an electron, kinetic energy E = p²/2m => p = √(2mE). De-Broglie wavelength λ_e = h/p = h/√(2mE). For a photon, energy E = hc/λ_ph => λ_ph = hc/E. The ratio λ_e/λ_ph = (h/√(2mE)) / (hc/E) = (h/√(2mE)) * (E/hc) = (1/c) * (E/√(2mE)) = (1/c) * √(E²/2mE) = (1/c) * √(E/(2m)).
Q21 | Mechanics Medium
ok conf:1.00
A disk and a sphere of same radius but different masses roll off on two inclined planes of the same altitude and length. Which one of the two objects gets to the bottom of the plane first?
(1) Disk
(2) Sphere ✓ Correct
(3) Both reach at the same time
(4) Depends on their masses
Solution: The acceleration of an object rolling down an inclined plane without slipping is given by a = (g sinθ) / (1 + k²/R²), where k is the radius of gyration and R is the radius. For a solid sphere, k²/R² = 2/5. So, a_sphere = (g sinθ) / (1 + 2/5) = (g sinθ) / (7/5) = (5/7)g sinθ. For a disc, k²/R² = 1/2. So, a_disc = (g sinθ) / (1 + 1/2) = (g sinθ) / (3/2) = (2/3)g sinθ. Comparing the accelerations: 5/7 ≈ 0.714 and 2/3 ≈ 0.667. Since a_sphere > a_disc, the sphere will reach the bottom first.
Q22 | Optics Easy
ok conf:1.00
The angle of incidence for a ray of light at a refracting surface of a prism is 45°. The angle of prism is 60°. If the ray suffers minimum deviation through the prism, the angle of minimum deviation and refractive index of the material of the prism respectively, are:
(1) 45°; 1/√2
(2) 30°; √2 ✓ Correct
(3) 45°; √2
(4) 30°; 1/√2
Solution: For minimum deviation in a prism, the angle of incidence (i₁) equals the angle of emergence (i₂) and the angle of refraction inside the prism (r₁) equals r₂. So i₁ = i₂ = 45°. The angle of minimum deviation is δ_min = i₁ + i₂ - A = 45° + 45° - 60° = 90° - 60° = 30°. The refractive index μ = sin((A + δ_min)/2) / sin(A/2) = sin((60° + 30°)/2) / sin(60°/2) = sin(45°) / sin(30°) = (1/√2) / (1/2) = √2. So, the angle of minimum deviation is 30° and the refractive index is √2.
Q23 | Modern physics Medium
ok conf:1.00
When an α-particle of mass ‘m’moving with velocity 'v' bombards on a heavy nucleus of charge 'Ze', its distance of closest approach from the nucleus depends on m as:
(1) 1/m ✓ Correct
(2) 1/√m
(3) 1/m²
(4) m
Solution: At the distance of closest approach (r₀), the initial kinetic energy of the α-particle is completely converted into electrostatic potential energy. So, (1/2)mv² = k * (Ze)(2e)/r₀, where k is Coulomb's constant. Rearranging for r₀: r₀ = (4kZe²)/(mv²). From this equation, it is clear that r₀ is inversely proportional to the mass m. So, r₀ ∝ 1/m.
Q24 | Mechanics Medium
ok conf:1.00
A particle of mass 10 g moves along a circle of radius 6.4 cm with a constant tangential acceleration. What is the magnitude of this acceleration if the kinetic energy of the particle becomes equal to 8 × 10⁻⁴ J by the end of the second revolution after the beginning of the motion?
(1) 0.1 m/s² ✓ Correct
(2) 0.15 m/s²
(3) 0.18 m/s²
(4) 0.2m
Solution: Mass m = 10 g = 0.01 kg. Radius r = 6.4 cm = 0.064 m. Kinetic energy KE = 8 × 10⁻⁴ J. Number of revolutions = 2, so angular displacement Δθ = 2 * 2π = 4π radians. Let a_t be the constant tangential acceleration. The angular acceleration α = a_t/r. From kinematics, ω² = ω₀² + 2αΔθ. Since the particle starts from rest, ω₀ = 0. So, ω² = 2(a_t/r)(4π) = 8πa_t/r. Kinetic energy KE = (1/2)mv² = (1/2)m(rω)² = (1/2)mr²ω². Substitute ω²: KE = (1/2)mr²(8πa_t/r) = 4πmra_t. Now, solve for a_t: a_t = KE / (4πmr). a_t = (8 × 10⁻⁴ J) / (4π * 0.01 kg * 0.064 m) = 0.0008 / (0.00256π) ≈ 0.0995 m/s² ≈ 0.1 m/s². C...
Q25 | Thermal physics Hard
ok conf:1.00
The molecules of a given mass of a gas have r.m.s velocity of 200 ms⁻¹ at 27°C and 1.0 × 10⁵ Nm⁻² pressure. When the temperature and pressure of the gas are respectively, 127°C and 0.05 × 10⁵ Nm⁻², the r.m.s. velocity of its molecules in ms⁻¹ is:
(1) 100√2
(2) 400/√3 ✓ Correct
(3) 100√2/√3
(4) 100/3
Solution: The RMS speed of gas molecules is given by V_rms = √(3RT/M), where T is the absolute temperature and M is the molar mass of the gas. This equation shows that V_rms depends only on temperature and molar mass, not on pressure (for a given gas). Given initial temperature T₁ = 27°C = 300 K and V₁ = 200 m/s. Final temperature T₂ = 127°C = 400 K. We need to find V₂. V_rms ∝ √T. So, V₂/V₁ = √(T₂/T₁). V₂ = V₁ * √(T₂/T₁) = 200 m/s * √(400 K / 300 K) = 200 * √(4/3) = 200 * (2/√3) = 400/√3 m/s.
Q26 | Electromagnetism Medium
ok conf:1.00
A long straight wire of radius a carries a steady current I. the current is uniformly distributed over its cross section. The ratio of the magnetic fields B and B', at radial distances a/2 and 2a respectively, from the axis of the wire is:
(1) 1/4
(2) 1/2
(3) 1 ✓ Correct
(4) 4
Solution: For a long straight wire with uniformly distributed current I and radius a: 1. Inside the wire (r < a): The magnetic field B_in = (µ₀Ir)/(2πa²). At r = a/2, B = (µ₀I(a/2))/(2πa²) = (µ₀I)/(4πa). 2. Outside the wire (r > a): The magnetic field B_out = (µ₀I)/(2πr). At r = 2a, B' = (µ₀I)/(2π(2a)) = (µ₀I)/(4πa). The ratio B/B' = ((µ₀I)/(4πa)) / ((µ₀I)/(4πa)) = 1.
Q27 | Mechanics Easy
ok conf:1.00
A particle moves so that its position vector is given by r = cos ωt î + sinωt ĵ. Where ω is a constant. Which of the following is true?
(1) Velocity and acceleration both are perpendicular to r.
(2) Velocity and acceleration both are parallel to r.
(3) Velocity is perpendicular to r and acceleration is directed towards the origin. ✓ Correct
(4) Velocity is perpendicular to r and acceleration is directed away from the origin.
Solution: Position vector r = (cos ωt)î + (sin ωt)ĵ. Velocity vector v = dr/dt = (-ω sin ωt)î + (ω cos ωt)ĵ. Dot product r ⋅ v = (cos ωt)(-ω sin ωt) + (sin ωt)(ω cos ωt) = -ω cos ωt sin ωt + ω sin ωt cos ωt = 0. Since r ⋅ v = 0, velocity is perpendicular to the position vector. Acceleration vector a = dv/dt = (-ω² cos ωt)î + (-ω² sin ωt)ĵ = -ω²[(cos ωt)î + (sin ωt)ĵ] = -ω²r. Since a = -ω²r, acceleration is always directed opposite to the position vector, i.e., towards the origin.
Q28 | Mechanics Easy
ok conf:1.00
What is the minimum velocity with which a body of mass m must enter a vertical loop of radius R so that it can complete the loop?
(1) √gR
(2) √2gR
(3) √3gR
(4) √5gR ✓ Correct
Solution: The minimum velocity required at the lowest point of a vertical loop for a body to complete the loop is v = √(5gR).
Q29 | Modern physics Medium
ok conf:1.00
When a metallic surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength λ, the stopping potential is V. If the same surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength 2λ, the stopping potential is V/4. The threshold wavelength for the metallic surface is:
(1) 4λ
(2) 5λ
(3) 5λ/2
(4) 3λ ✓ Correct
Solution: According to Einstein's photoelectric equation: eV_s = hc/λ - Φ, where Φ = hc/λ_th is the work function and λ_th is the threshold wavelength. Case 1: eV = hc/λ - hc/λ_th ...(i). Case 2: e(V/4) = hc/(2λ) - hc/λ_th ...(ii). From (i), V = (hc/e)(1/λ - 1/λ_th). Substitute this V into (ii): e[(hc/e)(1/λ - 1/λ_th)/4] = hc/(2λ) - hc/λ_th. (hc/4)(1/λ - 1/λ_th) = hc/(2λ) - hc/λ_th. Divide by hc: (1/4λ) - (1/4λ_th) = (1/2λ) - (1/λ_th). (1/λ_th) - (1/4λ_th) = (1/2λ) - (1/4λ). (3/4λ_th) = (1/4λ). 3/λ_th = 1/λ. λ_th = 3λ.
Q30 | Thermal physics Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A gas is compressed isothermally to half its initial volume. The same gas is compressed separately through an adiabatic process until its volume is again reduced to half. Then: [DIAGRAM]
(1) Compressing the gas isothermally will require more work to be done.
(2) Compressing the gas through adiabatic process will require more work to be done. ✓ Correct
(3) Compressing the gas isothermally or adiabatically will require the same amount of work.
(4) Which of the case (whether compression through isothermal or through adiabatic process) requires more work will depend upon the atomicity of the gas.
Solution: In a P-V diagram, the work done in compression is represented by the area under the P-V curve. For a given change in volume, the curve for an adiabatic process (PV^γ = constant) is steeper than the curve for an isothermal process (PV = constant). Therefore, for the same reduction in volume, the area under the adiabatic compression curve will be greater than the area under the isothermal compression curve. This means more work is done during adiabatic compression. [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q31 | Electromagnetism Easydiagram
ok conf:1.00
A potentiometer wire is 100 cm long and a constant potential difference is maintained across it. Two cells are connected in series first to support one another and then in opposite direction. The balance points are obtained at 50 cm and 10 cm from the positive end of the wire in the two cases. The ratio of emf's is: [DIAGRAM]
(1) 5:1
(2) 5:4
(3) 3:4
(4) 3:2 ✓ Correct
Solution: Let the emfs of the two cells be E₁ and E₂, and let the potential gradient of the potentiometer wire be k. When the cells are connected in series to support each other, their combined emf is E₁ + E₂. The balance point is at L₁ = 50 cm. So, E₁ + E₂ = kL₁ = 50k. When the cells are connected in series in opposite direction, their combined emf is |E₁ - E₂|. The balance point is at L₂ = 10 cm. So, |E₁ - E₂| = kL₂ = 10k. Assuming E₁ > E₂, we have E₁ - E₂ = 10k. Now, we have two equations: 1) E₁ + E₂ = 50k; 2) E₁ - E₂ = 10k. Adding (1) and (2): 2E₁ = 60k => E₁ = 30k. Subtracting (2) from (1): 2E₂ = 4...
Q32 | Optics Easy
ok conf:1.00
A astronomical telescope has objective and eyepiece of focal lengths 40 cm and 4 cm respectively. To view an object 200 cm away from the objective, the lenses must be separated by a distance:
(1) 37.3 cm
(2) 46.0 cm
(3) 50.0 cm
(4) 54.0 cm ✓ Correct
Solution: Focal length of objective f₀ = 40 cm. Focal length of eyepiece f_e = 4 cm. Object distance from objective u₀ = -200 cm. For the objective lens, using the lens formula 1/v₀ - 1/u₀ = 1/f₀: 1/v₀ - 1/(-200) = 1/40. 1/v₀ + 1/200 = 1/40. 1/v₀ = 1/40 - 1/200 = (5 - 1)/200 = 4/200 = 1/50. So, v₀ = 50 cm. For viewing with a relaxed eye, the final image is formed at infinity. This means the image formed by the objective (which acts as the object for the eyepiece) must be at the focal point of the eyepiece. So, the distance of the objective's image from the eyepiece |u_e| = f_e = 4 cm. The separation bet...
[solution diagram]

Q33 | Solid and fluid mechanics Harddiagram
ok conf:1.00
Two non-mixing liquids of densities ρ and nρ (n > 1) are put in a container. The height of each liquid is h. A solid cylinder of length L and density d is put in this container. The cylinder floats with its axis vertical and length pL (p < 1) in the denser liquid. The density d is equal to: [DIAGRAM]
(1) {1 + (n + 1)p}ρ
(2) {2 + (n + 1)p}ρ
(3) {2 + (n - 1)p}ρ
(4) {1 + (n - 1)p}ρ ✓ Correct
Solution: Let the cross-sectional area of the cylinder be A. The weight of the cylinder is W = (Volume) * d * g = (A * L) * d * g. The cylinder floats, so its weight equals the total buoyant force. The length pL is immersed in the denser liquid (density nρ), and the remaining length (L - pL) is immersed in the less dense liquid (density ρ). Total buoyant force F_b = (Volume in less dense liquid * ρ * g) + (Volume in denser liquid * nρ * g). F_b = (A * (L - pL) * ρ * g) + (A * pL * nρ * g). Equating weight and buoyant force: A * L * d * g = A * ρ * g * (L - pL + n * pL). Dividing by A * L * g: d = ρ * (1...
[solution diagram]

Q34 | Modern physics Easydiagram
ok conf:1.00
To get output 1 for the following circuit, the correct choice for the input is: [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) A = 0, B = 1, C = 0
(2) A = 1, B = 0, C = 0
(3) A = 1, B = 1, C = 0
(4) A = 1, B = 0, C = 1 ✓ Correct
Solution: The circuit diagram in the question shows an AND gate with inputs A and B. Its output (A AND B) feeds into a NAND gate. Input C also feeds into the same NAND gate. So, the final output Y = ((A AND B) AND C)'. We want Y = 1, which means ((A AND B) AND C) must be 0. Let's check option (4) as indicated by the solution: A=1, B=0, C=1. (A AND B) = (1 AND 0) = 0. ((A AND B) AND C) = (0 AND 1) = 0. So, Y = 0' = 1. This matches the requirement. The solution diagram seems to imply a different circuit (3-input AND then NOT), but the solution text points to option 4 explicitly. [DIAGRAM]
[solution diagram]

Q35 | Thermal physics Medium
ok conf:1.00
A piece of ice falls from a height h so that it melts completely. Only one-quarter of the heat produced is absorbed by the ice and all energy of ice gets converted into heat during its fall. The value of h is: [Latent heat of ice is 3.4 × 10⁵ J/kg and g = 10 N/kg]
(1) 34 km
(2) 544 km
(3) 136 km ✓ Correct
(4) 68 km
Solution: When a piece of ice of mass m falls from height h, the potential energy lost is mgh. This energy is converted into heat. Only one-quarter of this heat is absorbed by the ice to melt it. The heat required to melt mass m of ice is mL, where L is the latent heat of ice. So, (1/4)mgh = mL. We can cancel m from both sides: (1/4)gh = L. Solving for h: h = 4L/g. Given L = 3.4 × 10⁵ J/kg and g = 10 N/kg (or m/s²). h = (4 * 3.4 × 10⁵ J/kg) / (10 N/kg) = 13.6 × 10⁵ / 10 m = 1.36 × 10⁵ m = 136 km.
Q36 | Gravitation Hard
ok conf:1.00
The ratio of escape velocity at earth (v_e) to the escape velocity at a planet (v_p) whose radius and mean density are twice as that of earth is:
(1) 1:2
(2) 1:2√2 ✓ Correct
(3) 1:4
(4) 1:√2
Solution: Escape velocity v = √(2GM/R). We know that mass M = (4/3)πR³ρ, where ρ is the mean density. Substitute M in the escape velocity formula: v = √(2G * (4/3)πR³ρ / R) = √( (8/3)πGρR² ) = R√(8πGρ/3). Thus, escape velocity is proportional to R√ρ. For Earth: v_e ∝ R_e√(ρ_e). For the planet: R_p = 2R_e and ρ_p = 2ρ_e. So, v_p ∝ R_p√(ρ_p) = (2R_e)√(2ρ_e) = 2√2 R_e√(ρ_e). The ratio v_e/v_p = (R_e√(ρ_e)) / (2√2 R_e√(ρ_e)) = 1/(2√2). So the ratio is 1 : 2√2.
Q37 | basics and laboratories Easy
ok conf:1.00
If the magnitude of sum of two vectors is equal to the magnitude of difference of the two vectors, the angle between these vectors is:
(1) 0°
(2) 90° ✓ Correct
(3) 45°
(4) 180°
Solution: Let the two vectors be A and B. Given |A + B| = |A - B|. Square both sides: |A + B|² = |A - B|². A² + B² + 2AB cosθ = A² + B² - 2AB cosθ. 2AB cosθ = -2AB cosθ. 4AB cosθ = 0. If A and B are non-zero vectors, then cosθ must be 0. Therefore, θ = 90°.
Q38 | Modern physics Medium
ok conf:1.00
Given the value of Rydberg constant is 10⁷ m⁻¹, the wave number of the last line of the Balmer series in hydrogen spectrum will be:
(1) 0.025 × 10⁴ m⁻¹
(2) 0.5 × 10⁷ m⁻¹
(3) 0.25 × 10⁷ m⁻¹ ✓ Correct
(4) 2.5 × 10⁷ m⁻¹
Solution: The wave number (1/λ) for the hydrogen spectrum is given by Rydberg's formula: 1/λ = R (1/n₁² - 1/n₂²). For the Balmer series, the electron transitions to the second energy level, so n₁ = 2. For the last line of the Balmer series, the electron transitions from infinity, so n₂ = ∞. Therefore, 1/λ = R (1/2² - 1/∞²) = R (1/4 - 0) = R/4. Given R = 10⁷ m⁻¹. So, 1/λ = (10⁷ m⁻¹) / 4 = 0.25 × 10⁷ m⁻¹.
Q39 | Mechanics Hard
ok conf:1.00
A body of mass 1 kg begins to move under the action of a time dependent force F =(2tî +3t²ĵ) N, where î and ĵ are unit vectors along x and y axis. What power will be developed by the force at the time t?
(1) (2t² + 3t³) W
(2) (2t² + 4t⁴) W
(3) (2t³ + 3t⁴) W
(4) (2t³ + 3t⁵) W ✓ Correct
Solution: Given force F = (2t)î + (3t²)ĵ N. Mass m = 1 kg. Since F = ma, and m=1, acceleration a = (2t)î + (3t²)ĵ m/s². The body begins to move from rest, so initial velocity v₀ = 0. Velocity v = ∫a dt = ∫((2t)î + (3t²)ĵ) dt = (t²)î + (t³)ĵ m/s. Power P = F ⋅ v = ((2t)î + (3t²)ĵ) ⋅ ((t²)î + (t³)ĵ) = (2t)(t²) + (3t²)(t³) = 2t³ + 3t⁵ W.
Q40 | Electromagnetism Medium
ok conf:1.00
An inductor 20 mH, a capacitor 50 µF and a resistor 40 Ω are connected in series across a source of emf V = 10 sin 340 t. The power loss in A.C. circuit is :
(1) 0.51 W ✓ Correct
(2) 0.67 W
(3) 0.76 W
(4) 0.89 W
Solution: L = 20 mH = 20 × 10⁻³ H. C = 50 µF = 50 × 10⁻⁶ F. R = 40 Ω. The source emf is V = 10 sin(340 t), so the angular frequency ω = 340 rad/s and peak voltage V₀ = 10 V. Inductive reactance X_L = ωL = 340 * 20 × 10⁻³ = 6.8 Ω. Capacitive reactance X_C = 1/(ωC) = 1/(340 * 50 × 10⁻⁶) = 1/(0.017) ≈ 58.82 Ω. Impedance Z = √[R² + (X_L - X_C)²] = √[40² + (6.8 - 58.82)²] = √[1600 + (-52.02)²] = √[1600 + 2706.08] = √4306.08 ≈ 65.62 Ω. The RMS current I_rms = V_rms / Z = (V₀/√2) / Z = (10/√2) / 65.62 ≈ 7.07 / 65.62 ≈ 0.1077 A. The power loss in an AC circuit is P = I_rms²R = (0.1077)² * 40 ≈ 0.0116 * 40 ≈ 0.4...
Q41 | Mechanics Medium
ok conf:1.00
If the velocity of a particle is u = At + Bt², where A and B are constants, then the distance travelled by it between 1s and 2s is:
(1) (3/2)A + 4B
(2) 3A + 7B
(3) (3/2)A + (7/3)B ✓ Correct
(4) (2/3)A + (3/2)B
Solution: Given velocity v = At + Bt². The distance travelled x = ∫v dt. Integrating the velocity function: x(t) = ∫(At + Bt²) dt = (1/2)At² + (1/3)Bt³ + C (where C is the integration constant, which can be taken as 0 if we consider displacement from origin at t=0). Distance at t=1s: x(1) = (1/2)A(1)² + (1/3)B(1)³ = A/2 + B/3. Distance at t=2s: x(2) = (1/2)A(2)² + (1/3)B(2)³ = (1/2)A(4) + (1/3)B(8) = 2A + 8B/3. The distance travelled between 1s and 2s = x(2) - x(1) = (2A + 8B/3) - (A/2 + B/3) = (2A - A/2) + (8B/3 - B/3) = (4A - A)/2 + (8B - B)/3 = (3/2)A + (7/3)B.
Q42 | Electromagnetism Easy
ok conf:1.00
A long solenoid has 1000 turns. When a current of 4A flows through it the magnetic flux linked with each turn of the solenoid is 4 x 10⁻³ Wb. The self-inductance of the solenoid is:
(1) 4 H
(2) 3 H
(3) 2 H
(4) 1H ✓ Correct
Solution: Number of turns N = 1000. Current I = 4A. Magnetic flux linked with each turn Φ_turn = 4 × 10⁻³ Wb. The total magnetic flux linked with the solenoid is Φ_total = N * Φ_turn = 1000 * (4 × 10⁻³ Wb) = 4 Wb. The self-inductance L is defined by the relation Φ_total = LI. So, L = Φ_total / I = 4 Wb / 4 A = 1 H.
Q43 | Electormagneitsm Easy
ok conf:1.00
A small signal voltage V(t) = V₀ sin ωt is applied across an ideal capacitor C:
(1) Current I(t), lags voltage V(t) by 90°
(2) Over a full cycle the capacitor C does not consume any energy from the voltage source ✓ Correct
(3) Current I(t) is in phase with voltage V(t).
(4) Current I(t) leads voltage V(t) by 180°
Solution: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current I(t) leads the voltage V(t) by a phase angle of 90° (π/2 radians). The average power dissipated in an ideal capacitor over a full cycle is given by P_avg = V_rms I_rms cosφ. Since the phase difference φ = 90°, cosφ = cos(90°) = 0. Therefore, P_avg = 0. This means an ideal capacitor does not consume any energy over a full cycle.
Q44 | Optics Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Match the corresponding entries of column 1 with column 2. [Where m is the magnification produced by the mirror] [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) A → b and c; B→ b and c; C→ b and d; D→ a and d ✓ Correct
(2) A → a and c; B→ a and d; d→ a and b; D→ c and d
(3) A → a and d; B→ b and c; C→ b and d; D→ b and c
(4) A → c and d; B→ b and d; C→ b and c; D→ a and d
Solution: Properties of mirrors based on magnification m: - Magnification m = -v/u. - Real image is inverted, so m < 0. Virtual image is erect, so m > 0. - Magnified image |m| > 1. Diminished image |m| < 1. - Concave mirror forms real and inverted images (m<0) as well as virtual and erect (m>0, |m|>1). - Convex mirror always forms virtual, erect, and diminished images (m>0, |m|<1). (A) m = -2: Negative magnification means real and inverted image (c). Magnitude |m|=2 > 1 means magnified. Only a concave mirror (b) can form a real, inverted, magnified image. So A → b and c. (B) m = 1/2: Positive magnificat...
Q45 | Mechanics Harddiagram
ok conf:1.00
A car is negotiating a curved road of radius R. The road is banked at an angle θ. The coefficient of friction between the tyres of the car and the road is µ_s. The maximum safe velocity on this road is: [DIAGRAM]
(1) √(Rg * (µ_s + tanθ) / (1 - µ_s tanθ))
(2) √(Rg * (µ_s + tanθ) / (1 - µ_s tanθ)) ✓ Correct
(3) √(g * (µ_s + tanθ) / (R * (1 - µ_s tanθ)))
(4) √(g * (µ_s + tanθ) / (R² * (1 - µ_s tanθ)))
Solution: For maximum safe velocity, the frictional force f_s acts down the incline. The forces acting on the car are: Normal force N, weight mg, and frictional force f_s. Resolving forces: 1. Vertically: N cosθ - f_s sinθ = mg. 2. Horizontally (towards center of curve): N sinθ + f_s cosθ = mv²/R. For maximum velocity, f_s = µ_s N. Substitute f_s in the equations: 1. N cosθ - µ_s N sinθ = mg => N(cosθ - µ_s sinθ) = mg => N = mg / (cosθ - µ_s sinθ). 2. N sinθ + µ_s N cosθ = mv²/R => N(sinθ + µ_s cosθ) = mv²/R. Substitute N from (1) into (2): [mg / (cosθ - µ_s sinθ)] * (sinθ + µ_s cosθ) = mv²/R. g * (sinθ...
[solution diagram]

Q136 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Hot concentrated sulphuric acid is a moderately strong oxidizing agent. Which of the following reactions does not show oxidizing behaviour ?
(1) C + 2H2SO4 → CO2 + 2SO2 + 2H2O
(2) CaF2 + H2SO4 → CaSO4 + 2HF ✓ Correct
(3) Cu + 2H2SO4 → CuSO4 + SO2 + 2H2O
(4) 3S + 2H2SO4 → 3SO2 + 2H2O
Solution: CaF2 + H2SO4 → CaSO4 + 2HF In this reaction, oxidation number of none of the atom is not changed. Hence H2SO4 is not acting as oxidising agent.
Q137 | Inorganic Chemistry Easydiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following pairs of d-orbitals will have electron density along the axes ?
(1) d_z^2, d_x^2-y^2 ✓ Correct
(2) d_xy, d_x^2-y^2
(3) d_z^2, d_xz
(4) d_xz, d_yz
Solution: d_z^2 and d_x^2-y^2 has electron density concentrated on the axis.
[solution diagram]

Q138 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.80
The correct geometry and hybridizatioon for XeF4 are :
(1) Planar triangle, sp³d³
(2) Square planar, sp³d²
(3) Octahedral, sp³d² ✓ Correct
(4) Trigonal bipyramidal, sp³d
Solution: XeF4, AB4L2→ sp³d2 → geometry → octahedral → shape → square planar. (Note: The solution text describes the shape as Square planar and hybridization as sp3d2, which corresponds to option (2). The marked answer (3) has Octahedral, sp3d2 which is the electron geometry, not the molecular geometry (shape). Based on chemical principles, option (2) is correct.)
Q139 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Among the following which one is a wrong statement ?
(1) SeF4 and CH4 have same shape ✓ Correct
(2) I_3+ has bent geometry
(3) PH5 and BiCl5 do not exist
(4) pπ-dπ bonds are present in SO2
Solution: SeF4 -sp³d, lp = 1, shape = see-saw. CH4 -sp³, lp = 0, shape = tetrahedral. Since shapes are different, statement (1) is wrong.
Q140 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The correct increasing order of trans-effect of the following species is :
(1) Br¯ > CN¯ > NH3 > C6H5¯
(2) CN¯ > Br¯ > C6H5¯ >NH3
(3) NH3 > CN¯ > Br¯ > C6H5¯
(4) CN¯ > C6H5¯ > Br¯ > NH3. ✓ Correct
Solution: Trans effect order is NH3 < Br⁻ < C6H5⁻ < CN⁻. This corresponds to option (4) if read as increasing order from right to left.
Q141 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Which one of the following statements related to lanthanons is incorrect ?
(1) All the lanthanons are much more reactive than aluminium ✓ Correct
(2) Ce(+4) solutions are widely used as oxidizing agent in volumetric analysis
(3) Europium shows +2 oxidation state.
(4) The basicity decreases as the ionic radius decreases from Pr to Lu.
Solution: Lanthanon's are less reactive than aluminium due to high IP (Lanthanoid contraction). Ce⁺⁴ is good oxidising agent and easily converted into Ce⁺³. Eu(63) = 4f⁷ 5d⁰ 6s², Eu⁺² = 4f⁷. In lanthanoids series 'Ce' to Lu ionic radius regularly decreases and covalent character increase, basic character of hydroxide decrease. Therefore, statement (1) is incorrect.
Q142 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Jahn-Teller effect not observed in high spin complexes of :
(1) d4
(2) d9
(3) d7
(4) d8 ✓ Correct
Solution: Jahn-Teller effect explains axial distortion in unsymmetrical octahedral geometry. It is not found in high spin complexes of d⁸ configurations.
Q143 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following can be used as the halide component for Friedel-Crafts reaction?
(1) Chloroethene
(2) Isopropyl chloride ✓ Correct
(3) Chlorobenzene
(4) Bromobenzene
Solution: Isopropyl chloride can be used. In chlorobenzene, Bromobenzene, chloroethene, the lone pair of halogen are delocalised with π bonds, so they attain double bond character and are less reactive towards Friedel-Crafts reaction.
[solution diagram]

Q144 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
In which of the following molecules, all atoms are coplanar ?
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: For biphenyl, all atoms are coplanar when rotated to be planar. Structure (3) represents biphenyl, which can adopt a planar conformation where all atoms are coplanar.
[solution diagram]

Q145 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which one of the following structures represents nylon 6,6 polymer ?
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: Nylon 6,6 is a polyamide formed from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid. The structure of nylon 6,6 is [-NH(CH2)6NHCO(CH2)4CO-]n, which corresponds to option (2).
[solution diagram]

Q146 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
In pyrrole [DIAGRAM], the electron density is maximum on :
[question diagram]

(1) 2 and 4
(2) 2 and 5 ✓ Correct
(3) 2 and 3
(4) 3 and 4
Solution: Due to resonance in pyrrole, the nitrogen's lone pair is delocalized, leading to increased electron density at positions 2 and 5, as shown in the resonance structures.
[solution diagram]

Q147 | Organic Chemistry Harddiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following compounds shall not produced propene by reaction with HBr followed by elimination of direct only elimination reaction ?
[question diagram]

(1) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: The reaction of H2C=C=O (ketene) with HBr yields bromopropionic acid derivatives, not directly propene via elimination. Other options can form propene through HBr addition and subsequent elimination.
[solution diagram]

Q148 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which one of the following nitro-compounds does not react with nitrous acid ?
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: 3°-Nitro compounds do not react with HNO2 because of the absence of α-H atoms.
Q149 | Organic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The central dogma of molecular genetics states that the genetic information flows from :
(1) DNA → RNA → Proteins ✓ Correct
(2) DNA → RNA → Carbohydrates
(3) Amino acids → Proteins
(4) DNA → Carbohydrates → Proteins
Solution: The central dogma of molecular biology describes the flow of genetic information from DNA to RNA (transcription) and from RNA to proteins (translation).
Q150 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
The correct corresponding order names of four aldoses with configuration given below [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) L-erythrose, L-threose, D-erythrose, D-threose
(2) D-erythrose, D-threose, L-erythrose, L-threose ✓ Correct
(3) L-erythrose, L-threose, L-erythrose, D-threose
(4) D-threose, D-erythrose, L-threose, L-erythrose
Solution: The configuration of the aldoses determines their classification as D- or L- and their specific names (erythrose or threose). The given structures are identified as D-erythrose, D-threose, L-erythrose, and L-threose respectively.
[solution diagram]

Q151 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.80
In the given reaction : [DIAGRAM of Benzene + Isopropyl chloride in presence of HF/0°C]. The product P is:
[question diagram]

(1) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: The reaction shown is Friedel-Crafts alkylation. Benzene reacts with isopropyl chloride in the presence of HF (a Lewis acid catalyst, although AlCl3 is more common) to form isopropylbenzene, also known as cumene. Cumene is shown in option (2). The solution states (1) as the answer, but its diagram and reaction mechanism lead to cumene, which is (2). I will adhere to the provided solution's marked option.
[solution diagram]

Q152 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
A given nitrogen-containing aromatic compound A reacts with Sn/HCl, followed by HNO2 to give an unstable compound B. B, on treatment with phenol, forms a beautiful coloured compound C with the molecular formula C12H10N2O. The structure of compound A is :
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 4]

Solution: Compound A, nitrobenzene (option 4), on reduction with Sn/HCl gives aniline. Aniline reacts with HNO2 to form benzene diazonium chloride (B). Benzene diazonium chloride on treatment with phenol undergoes azo coupling to form p-hydroxy azobenzene (C, C12H10N2O), which is a colored compound.
[solution diagram]

Q153 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
Consider the reaction CH3CH2CH2Br + NaCN → CH3CH2CH2CN + NaBr. This reaction will be the fastest in :
(1) N, N’-dimethylformamide (DMF) ✓ Correct
(2) Water
(3) Ethanol
(4) Methanol
Solution: The reaction is a nucleophilic substitution (SN2 type) which is favored by polar aprotic solvents that solvate cations but not anions, enhancing nucleophilicity. N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) is a polar aprotic solvent, making the reaction fastest in it.
Q154 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.80
The correct structure of the product A formed in the reaction : [DIAGRAM of cyclohexanone undergoing reduction with H2/Pd/Carbon, ethanol to form A]
[question diagram]

(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 4]

Solution: The reaction is catalytic hydrogenation of cyclohexanone. Ketones are reduced to secondary alcohols. Therefore, cyclohexanone (starting material) is reduced to cyclohexanol. Cyclohexanol is represented by option (1). The solution marks (4) which is the starting material, implying no reaction, which is incorrect. I will adhere to the provided solution's marked option.
[solution diagram]

Q155 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
Which among the given molecules can exhibit tautomerism ? [DIAGRAM showing three molecules I, II, III]
[question diagram]

(1) Both I and II
(2) Both II and III
(3) III only ✓ Correct
(4) Both I and III
Solution: Tautomerism, specifically keto-enol tautomerism, requires an alpha-hydrogen. Only molecule III possesses an α-hydrogen (on the carbon adjacent to the carbonyl group) allowing for tautomerism. Molecules I and II do not have alpha hydrogens available for tautomerization.
[solution diagram]

Q156 | Organic Chemistry Mediumdiagram
ok conf:0.90
The correct order of strengths of the carboxylic acids [DIAGRAM showing three carboxylic acids (I: Benzoic acid, II: p-nitrobenzoic acid, III: p-methoxybenzoic acid)] Is :
[question diagram]

(1) III > II > I
(2) II > I > III
(3) I > II > III
(4) II > III > I ✓ Correct
Solution: Acidity of carboxylic acids is enhanced by electron-withdrawing groups (like -NO2) and reduced by electron-donating groups (like -OCH3). Thus, p-nitrobenzoic acid (II) is most acidic, followed by benzoic acid (I), and then p-methoxybenzoic acid (III) is the least acidic. Therefore, the order is II > I > III.
[solution diagram]

Q157 | Organic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
The compound that will react most readily with gaseous bromine has the formula :
[question diagram]

(1) C4H10
(2) C2H4
(3) C3H6 ✓ Correct
(4) C2H2
Solution: Alkenes react readily with gaseous bromine via electrophilic addition. Among the given options, propene (C3H6) is an alkene. The solution mentions allylic substituted product by free radical mechanism, which usually happens with high temperature or light, but direct reaction with Br2 typically refers to addition across the double bond.
Q158 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which one of the following compounds shows the presence of intramolecular hydrogen bond ?
(1) Cellulose ✓ Correct
(2) Concentrated acetic acid
(3) H2O2
(4) HCN
Solution: Cellulose exhibits intramolecular hydrogen bonding within its polymer chains. Acetic acid, H2O2, and HCN primarily show intermolecular hydrogen bonding.
Q159 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
The molar conductivity of a 0.5 mol/dm³ solution of AgNO3 with electrolytic conductivity of 5.76 × 10¯³ S cm¯¹ at 298 K is :
(1) 0.086 S cm²/mol
(2) 28.8 S cm²/mol
(3) 2.88 S cm²/mol
(4) 11.52 S cm²/mol ✓ Correct
Solution: Molar conductivity (Λm) = (kappa × 1000) / Molarity. Given kappa (κ) = 5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ and Molarity (C) = 0.5 mol/dm³ = 0.5 mol/L. Λm = (5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ × 1000 cm³/L) / 0.5 mol/L = 11.52 S cm²/mol.
Q160 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
The decomposition of phosphine (PH3) on tungsten at low pressure is a first-order reaction. It is because the :
(1) Rate is independent of the surface coverage
(2) Rate of decomposition is very slow
(3) Rate is proportional to the surface coverage ✓ Correct
(4) Rate is inversely proportional to the surface coverage
Solution: For heterogeneous reactions occurring on a surface, at low pressure, the surface coverage is proportional to the reactant concentration. Thus, the rate, being dependent on surface coverage, becomes first-order with respect to the gas-phase concentration.
Q161 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The coagulation values in millimoles per litre of the electrolytes used for the coagulation of As2S3 are given below : I. (NaCl) = 52, II. (BaCl2) = 0.69, III. (MgSO4) = 0.22 The correct order of their coagulating power is :
(1) III > II > I ✓ Correct
(2) III > I > II
(3) I > II > III
(4) II > I > III
Solution: Coagulation power is inversely proportional to the coagulation value. The lower the coagulation value, the higher the coagulating power. Given values: NaCl=52, BaCl2=0.69, MgSO4=0.22. Order of coagulating power: MgSO4 (III) > BaCl2 (II) > NaCl (I).
Q162 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
During the electrolysis of molten sodium chloride, the time required to produce 0.10 mol of chlorine gas using a current of 3 amperes is :
(1) 220 minutes
(2) 330 minutes
(3) 55 minutes
(4) 110 minutes ✓ Correct
Solution: The reaction for Cl2 production is 2Cl⁻ → Cl2(g) + 2e⁻. From Faraday's law, 1 mole of Cl2 (71g) requires 2 moles of electrons (2F). Moles of Cl2 = 0.1 mol, so mass = 0.1 × 71 = 7.1g. Charge (Q) = current (I) × time (t). Also, Q = (mass / equivalent weight) × Faraday's constant. Equivalent weight of Cl2 = 71/2 = 35.5 g/mol. So, 3A × t = (7.1g / 35.5 g/mol) × 96500 C/mol. t = (0.2 × 96500) / 3 = 6433.33 seconds. In minutes: 6433.33 / 60 ≈ 107.22 minutes, which is approximately 110 minutes.
Q163 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
How many electrons can fit in the orbital for which n = 3 and l =1 ?
(1) 10
(2) 14
(3) 2 ✓ Correct
(4) 6
Solution: For n = 3 and l = 1, this corresponds to a 3p orbital. Each orbital (regardless of its type) can hold a maximum of 2 electrons with opposite spins, according to Pauli's exclusion principle.
Q164 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
For a sample of perfect gas when its pressure is changed isothermally from pi to pf, the entropy change is given by :
(1) ΔS = nRT ln (Pf/Pi)
(2) ΔS = RT ln (Pf/Pi)
(3) ΔS = nR ln (Pi/Pf)
(4) ΔS = nR ln (Pf/Pi) ✓ Correct
Solution: For an ideal gas, the change in entropy (ΔS) for an isothermal process (constant temperature, Ti = Tf) is given by ΔS = nR ln(Vf/Vi) or ΔS = nR ln(Pi/Pf) if volume is changing, or ΔS = nR ln(Pf/Pi) if pressure is changing (since PiVi = PfVf => Vi/Vf = Pf/Pi). The solution correctly derives ΔS = nR ln(Pf/Pi).
Q165 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The van't Hoff factor (i) for a dilute aqueous solution of the strong electrolyte barium hydroxide is :
(1) 2
(2) 3 ✓ Correct
(3) 0
(4) 1
Solution: Barium hydroxide, Ba(OH)2, is a strong electrolyte and dissociates completely in water as Ba(OH)2(aq) → Ba²⁺(aq) + 2OH⁻(aq). For every one molecule of Ba(OH)2, three ions are produced (one Ba²⁺ and two OH⁻). Therefore, the van't Hoff factor (i) is 3.
Q166 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
The percentage of pyridine (C5H5N) that forms pyridinium ion (C5H5N⁺H) in a 0.10 M aqueous pyridine solution (Kb for C5H5N = 1.7 × 10¯⁹) is :
(1) 0.77%
(2) 1.6%
(3) 0.0060%
(4) 0.013% ✓ Correct
Solution: Pyridine (C5H5N) is a weak base. For a weak base, the degree of dissociation (α) can be calculated as α = √(Kb/C), where Kb is the base dissociation constant and C is the initial concentration. α = √(1.7 × 10⁻⁹ / 0.10) = √(1.7 × 10⁻⁸) = 1.30 × 10⁻⁴. Percentage α = α × 100 = 1.30 × 10⁻⁴ × 100 = 0.013%.
Q167 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
In calcium fluoride, having the fluorite structure, the coordination numbers for calcium ion (Ca²⁺) and fluoride ion (F¯) are :
(1) 8 and 4 ✓ Correct
(2) 4 and 8
(3) 4 and 2
(4) 6 and 6
Solution: In the fluorite structure (CaF2), each Ca²⁺ ion is surrounded by 8 F⁻ ions (cubic coordination), and each F⁻ ion is surrounded by 4 Ca²⁺ ions (tetrahedral coordination). Thus, the coordination numbers are 8 for Ca²⁺ and 4 for F⁻.
Q168 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
If the Ecell for a given reaction has a negative value, which of the following gives the correct relationships for the values of ΔG° and Keq?
[question diagram]


(1) ΔG° < 0; Keq > 1
(2) ΔG° < 0; Keq < 1
(3) ΔG° > 0; Keq < 1 ✓ Correct
(4) ΔG° > 0; Keq > 1
Solution: The relationship between standard cell potential (E°cell) and standard Gibbs free energy change (ΔG°) is ΔG° = -nFE°cell. If E°cell is negative, then ΔG° will be positive (ΔG° > 0). The relationship between ΔG° and equilibrium constant (Keq) is ΔG° = -RT ln Keq. If ΔG° > 0, then ln Keq must be negative, implying Keq < 1.
Q169 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which one of the following is incorrect for ideal solution ?
(1) ΔP = Pobserved - Pcalculated by Raoult's law = 0
(2) ΔGmix = 0 ✓ Correct
(3) ΔHmix = 0
(4) ΔUmix = 0
Solution: For an ideal solution, the enthalpy of mixing (ΔHmix) and change in volume of mixing (ΔVmix) are zero (ΔHmix = 0, ΔVmix = 0). Since ΔVmix = 0, ΔUmix = 0. Also, it obeys Raoult's law, so ΔP = 0. However, the Gibbs free energy of mixing (ΔGmix) for any spontaneous mixing process (including ideal solutions) must be negative (ΔGmix < 0), as mixing increases entropy (ΔSmix > 0). Therefore, ΔGmix = ΔHmix - TΔSmix = 0 - T(ΔSmix) = -TΔSmix ≠ 0. So, the statement ΔGmix = 0 is incorrect.
Q170 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
The solubility of AgCl(s) with solubility product 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ in 0.1 M NaCl solution would be :
[question diagram]


(1) 1.6 × 10⁻¹¹ M
(2) Zero
(3) 1.26 × 10⁻⁵ M
(4) 1.6 × 10⁻⁹ M ✓ Correct
Solution: AgCl(s) ⇌ Ag⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq). Let solubility of AgCl be S. From NaCl (0.1 M), [Cl⁻] = 0.1 M. So, [Cl⁻]total = S + 0.1 ≈ 0.1 M (since S is expected to be very small). Ksp = [Ag⁺][Cl⁻] = S × 0.1 = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰. Therefore, S = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ / 0.1 = 1.6 × 10⁻⁹ M.
Q171 | Physical Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Suppose the elements X and Y combine to form two compounds XY2 and X3Y2. When 0.1 mole of XY2 weighs 10 g and 0.05 mole of X3Y2 weighs 9 g, the atomic weights of X and Y are :
(1) 20, 30
(2) 30, 20
(3) 40, 30 ✓ Correct
(4) 60, 40
Solution: Let atomic weight of X be Ax and Y be Ay. For XY2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 10g / 0.1 mol = 100 g/mol. So, Ax + 2Ay = 100 (Eq 1). For X3Y2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 9g / 0.05 mol = 180 g/mol. So, 3Ax + 2Ay = 180 (Eq 2). Subtracting Eq 1 from Eq 2: (3Ax + 2Ay) - (Ax + 2Ay) = 180 - 100 => 2Ax = 80 => Ax = 40. Substituting Ax = 40 into Eq 1: 40 + 2Ay = 100 => 2Ay = 60 => Ay = 30. Thus, atomic weights are 40 and 30.
Q172 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The number of electrons delivered at the cathode during electrolysis by a current of 1 ampere in 60 seconds (charge on electron = 1.60 × 10⁻¹⁹ C)
(1) 3.75 × 10²⁰ ✓ Correct
(2) 7.48 × 10²³
(3) 6 × 10²³
(4) 6 × 10²⁰
Solution: Total charge (Q) passed = current (I) × time (t) = 1 A × 60 s = 60 C. Number of electrons (n) = Total charge (Q) / charge per electron (e) = 60 C / (1.60 × 10⁻¹⁹ C/electron) = 3.75 × 10²⁰ electrons.
Q173 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
Boric acid is an acid because its molecule :
[question diagram]

(1) Accepts OH⁻ from water releasing proton ✓ Correct
(2) Combines with proton from water molecule
(3) Contains replaceable H⁺ ion
(4) Gives up a proton
Solution: Boric acid (H3BO3 or B(OH)3) is a weak monobasic Lewis acid. It acts as an acid not by donating a proton, but by accepting a hydroxyl ion (OH⁻) from water, thereby releasing a proton from water. B(OH)₃ + H₂O ⇌ [B(OH)₄]⁻ + H⁺.
Q174 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
AlF3 is soluble in HF only in presence of KF. It is due to the formation of :
(1) AlH3
(2) K[AlF3H]
(3) K3[AlF3H3]
(4) K3[AlF6] ✓ Correct
Solution: AlF3 is an ionic compound but sparingly soluble. In the presence of KF, AlF3 reacts to form a soluble complex salt, potassium hexafluoroaluminate (K3[AlF6]). AlF3 + 3KF → K3[AlF6].
Q175 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
Zinc can be coated on iron to produce galvanized iron but the reverse is not possible. It is because :
(1) Zinc has lower negative electrode potential than iron
(2) Zinc has higher negative electrode potential than iron ✓ Correct
(3) Zinc is lighter than iron
(4) Zinc has lower melting point than iron
Solution: Galvanization (coating iron with zinc) protects iron because zinc is more reactive than iron (has a higher negative electrode potential, meaning it is more easily oxidized). This allows zinc to act as a sacrificial anode. The reverse coating is not possible because iron is less reactive and would not provide cathodic protection to zinc.
Q176 | Physical Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
The suspension of slaked lime in water is known as :
(1) Milk of lime ✓ Correct
(2) Aqueous solution of slaked lime
(3) Limewater
(4) Quicklime
Solution: Slaked lime is calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2). An aqueous solution of Ca(OH)2 is called limewater. A suspension of Ca(OH)2 in water is called milk of lime. Quicklime is calcium oxide (CaO).
Q177 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
low_confidence conf:0.70
The hybridizations of atomic orbital of nitrogen in NO2⁻, NO3⁻ and NH4⁺ respectively are
(1) sp, sp² and sp³ ✓ Correct
(2) sp², sp and sp³
(3) sp, sp³ and sp²
(4) sp², sp³ and sp
Solution: For NO2⁻: N is sp² hybridized (2 bonding domains + 1 lone pair = 3 electron domains, bent shape). For NO3⁻: N is sp² hybridized (3 bonding domains + 0 lone pair = 3 electron domains, trigonal planar shape). For NH4⁺: N is sp³ hybridized (4 bonding domains + 0 lone pair = 4 electron domains, tetrahedral shape). The correct sequence is sp², sp², sp³. However, the provided solution marks option (1) which is sp, sp², sp³. There is a discrepancy between the solution's explanation and its marked answer.
Q178 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following fluoro-compounds is most likely to behave as a Lewis base ?
(1) CF4
(2) SiF4
(3) BF3
(4) PF3 ✓ Correct
Solution: A Lewis base is a species that can donate a pair of electrons. Among the given compounds, PF3 has a lone pair of electrons on the phosphorus atom which it can donate, thus acting as a Lewis base. BF3 is a Lewis acid (electron deficient). CF4 and SiF4 have no lone pairs available for donation.
Q179 | Inorganic Chemistry Medium
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following pairs of ions is isoelectronic and isostructural ?
(1) SO3²⁻, CO3²⁻
(2) ClO3⁻, SO3²⁻ ✓ Correct
(3) CO3²⁻, NO3⁻
(4) ClO3⁻, CO3²⁻
Solution: Isoelectronic means having the same number of electrons. Isostructural means having the same structure (shape). For ClO3⁻: electrons = 17(Cl) + 3*8(O) + 1(charge) = 42. Shape is pyramidal (sp³ with one lone pair). For SO3²⁻: electrons = 16(S) + 3*8(O) + 2(charge) = 42. Shape is pyramidal (sp³ with one lone pair). Thus, ClO3⁻ and SO3²⁻ are both isoelectronic and isostructural.
Q180 | Inorganic Chemistry Easy
ok conf:0.90
In context with beryllium, which one of the following statements is incorrect ?
(1) Its salts rarely hydrolyze ✓ Correct
(2) Its hydride is electron-deficient and polymeric
(3) It is rendered passive by nitric acid
(4) It forms Be2C
Solution: Beryllium salts (e.g., BeCl2, BeSO4) are covalent in nature and hence undergo hydrolysis readily in water due to high charge density and small size of Be²⁺ ion. Therefore, the statement 'Its salts rarely hydrolyze' is incorrect.
Q91 | Organisms and Populations Medium
ok conf:0.90
Gause's principle of competitive exclusion states that (1) More abundant species will exclude the less abundant species through competition. (2) Competition for the same resources excludes species having different food preferences. (3) No two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely for the same limited resources. (4) Larger organisms exclude smaller ones through competition.
(1) More abundant species will exclude the less abundant species through competition.
(2) Competition for the same resources excludes species having different food preferences.
(3) No two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely for the same limited resources. ✓ Correct
(4) Larger organisms exclude smaller ones through competition.
Solution: Gause's ‘Competitive Exclusion Principle' states that two closely related species competing for the same resources cannot coexist indefinitely and the competitively inferior one will be eliminated eventually.
Q92 | Biotechnology and its Applications Medium
ok conf:0.90
The two polypeptides of human insulin are linked together by (1) Hydrogen bonds (2) Phosphodiester bond (3) Covalent bond (4) Disulphide bridges
(1) Hydrogen bonds
(2) Phosphodiester bond
(3) Covalent bond
(4) Disulphide bridges ✓ Correct
Solution: The two polypeptides called A peptide and B peptide of human insulin were produced separately using plasmids of E.coli and were linked together by disulphide bonds in vitro.
Q93 | Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:0.90
The coconut water from tender coconut represents (1) Endocarp (2) Fleshy mesocarp (3) Free nuclear proembryo (4) Free nuclear endosperm
(1) Endocarp
(2) Fleshy mesocarp
(3) Free nuclear proembryo
(4) Free nuclear endosperm ✓ Correct
Solution: Endosperm development precedes embryo development. The most common type of endosperm development is free-nuclear endosperm where the PEN undergoes successive divisions without immediate cell wall formation after each division. Later on cell walls are laid between the nuclei turning it into cellular endosperm.
Q94 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following statements is wrong for viroids? (1) They lack a protein coat (2) They are smaller than viruses (3) They cause infections (4) Their RNA is of high molecular weight
(1) They lack a protein coat
(2) They are smaller than viruses
(3) They cause infections
(4) Their RNA is of high molecular weight ✓ Correct
Solution: The RNA of viroids has low molecular weight as it lacks a protein coat around it.
Q95 | Animal Kingdom Medium
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following features is not present in the Phylum - Arthropoda? (1) Chitinous exoskeleton (2) Metameric segmentation (3) Parapodia (4) Jointed appendages
(1) Chitinous exoskeleton
(2) Metameric segmentation
(3) Parapodia ✓ Correct
(4) Jointed appendages
Solution: Parapodia are lateral appendages found in aquatic annelids which help in swimming.
Q96 | Principles of Inheritance and Variation Medium
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following most appropriately describes haemophilia? (1) Recessive gene disorder (2) X - linked recessive gene disorder (3) Chromosomal disorder (4) Dominant gene disorder
(1) Recessive gene disorder
(2) X - linked recessive gene disorder ✓ Correct
(3) Chromosomal disorder
(4) Dominant gene disorder
Solution: Haemophilia is a sex linked recessive disease. The defective gene is present on the X chromosome of the 23rd pair but is recessive in nature. The heterozygous female (carrier) for haemophilia may transmit the disease to sons. The haemophilic male transmits the defective gene to the daughters.
Q97 | Photosynthesis Medium
ok conf:0.90
Emerson's enhancement effect and Red drop has been instrumental in the discovery of (1) Photophosphorylation and non-cyclic electron transport (2) Two photosystems operating simultaneously (3) Photophosphorylation and cyclic electron transport (4) Oxidative phosphorylation
(1) Photophosphorylation and non-cyclic electron transport
(2) Two photosystems operating simultaneously ✓ Correct
(3) Photophosphorylation and cyclic electron transport
(4) Oxidative phosphorylation
Solution: Emerson's enhancement effect is the increase in the rate of photosynthesis after the chloroplast were exposed to light of wavelength 680 nm and more than 680 nm (far red spectrum) simultaneously. This proved the existence of two photosystems in plants. He also proved that when the chloroplasts were exposed to only the monochromatic light of more than 680 nm (far red spectrum), there was a decrease in the photosynthetic rate which is known as red drop.
Q98 | Mineral Nutrition Medium
ok conf:0.90
In which of the following, all three are macronutrients? (1) Boron, zinc, manganese (2) Iron, copper, molybdenum (3) Molybdenum, magnesium, manganese (4) Nitrogen, nickel, phosphorus
(1) Boron, zinc, manganese ✓ Correct
(2) Iron, copper, molybdenum
(3) Molybdenum, magnesium, manganese
(4) Nitrogen, nickel, phosphorus
Solution: In none of the groups all the elements are macronutrients.
Q99 | Breathing and Exchange of Gases Easy
ok conf:0.90
Name the chronic respiratory disorder caused mainly by cigarette smoking (1) Emphysema (2) Asthma (3) Respiratory acidosis (4) Respiratory alkalosis
(1) Emphysema ✓ Correct
(2) Asthma
(3) Respiratory acidosis
(4) Respiratory alkalosis
Solution: Emphysema is caused by excessive cigarette smoking. In this disorder the alveolar walls get damaged and become stiff, so the surface area for exchanges of gases reduces tremendously.
Q100 | Environmental Issues Easy
ok conf:0.90
A system of rotating crops with legume or grass pasture to improve soil structure and fertility is called (1) Ley farming (3) Strip farming (2) Contour farming (4) Shifting agriculture
(1) Ley farming ✓ Correct
(2) Contour farming
(3) Strip farming
(4) Shifting agriculture
Solution: Ley farming is used to improve soil structure in a natural way.
Q101 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:0.90
Mitochondria and chloroplast are (a) semi-autonomous organelles (b) formed by division of pre-existing organelles and they contain DNA but lack protein synthesizing machinery. Which one of the following options is correct? (1) Both (a) and (b) are correct (3) (a) is true but (b) is false (2) (b) is true but (a) is false (4) Both (a) and (b) are false
(1) Both (a) and (b) are correct
(2) (b) is true but (a) is false
(3) (a) is true but (b) is false ✓ Correct
(4) Both (a) and (b) are false
Solution: 70 S ribosomes and RNA are present in both mitochondria and chloroplasts which are required for protein synthesis.
Q102 | Reproductive Health Easy
ok conf:0.90
In the context of Amniocentesis, which of the following statements is incorrect? (1) It is usually done when a woman is between 14-16 weeks pregnant. (2) It is used for prenatal sex determination (3) It can be used for detection of Down syndrome (4) It can be used for detection of Cleft palate
(1) It is usually done when a woman is between 14-16 weeks pregnant.
(2) It is used for prenatal sex determination
(3) It can be used for detection of Down syndrome
(4) It can be used for detection of Cleft palate ✓ Correct
Solution: Detection of Cleft palate can be done by ultrasound as early as 16 weeks into a pregnancy.
Q103 | Photosynthesis in Higher Plants Hard
ok conf:0.90
In a chloroplast the highest number of protons are found in (1) Stroma (3) Inter membrane space (2) Lumen of thylakoids (4) Antennae complex
(1) Stroma
(2) Lumen of thylakoids ✓ Correct
(3) Inter membrane space
(4) Antennae complex
Solution: Splitting of water molecules takes place on the inner side of the membrane so the protons of hydrogen ions produced by the splitting accumulate within the lumen of thylakoids.
Q104 | Neural Control and Coordination Medium
ok conf:0.90
Photosensitive compound in human eye is made up of (1) Guanosine and Retinol (3) Opsin and Retinol (2) Opsin and Retinal (4) Transducin and Retinene
(1) Guanosine and Retinol
(2) Opsin and Retinal ✓ Correct
(3) Opsin and Retinol
(4) Transducin and Retinene
Solution: Photosensitive compound in the human eye is made up of a purplish-red protein called rhodopsin which contains a derivative of Vitamin A. These are located on the outermost layer of the retina.
Q105 | Cell Cycle and Cell Division Easy
ok conf:0.90
Spindle fibres attach on to (1) Telomere of the chromosome (2) Kinetochore of the chromosome (3) Centromere of the chromosome (4) Kinetosome of the chromosome
(1) Telomere of the chromosome
(2) Kinetochore of the chromosome ✓ Correct
(3) Centromere of the chromosome
(4) Kinetosome of the chromosome
Solution: Kinetochores are small disc-like structures present on the surface of the centromere and these are the sites for the attachment of spindle fibres so that the chromatids can be pulled to opposite poles during anaphase stage.
Q106 | Animal Kingdom Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which is the National Aquatic Animal of India? (1) Gangetic shark (2) River dolphin (3) Blue whale (4) Seahorse
(1) Gangetic shark
(2) River dolphin ✓ Correct
(3) Blue whale
(4) Seahorse
Solution: To save the dolphins from extinction the government of India has declared the river dolphin, the national aquatic animal of India.
Q107 | Molecular Basis of Inheritance Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following is required as inducer(s) for the expression of Lac operon? (1) Glucose (3) Lactose (2) Galactose (4) Lactose and galactose
(1) Glucose
(2) Galactose
(3) Lactose ✓ Correct
(4) Lactose and galactose
Solution: The Lac operon in E. coli. bacteria (prokaryote) remain off but if lactose is added in its surrounding medium the operon turns on and starts metabolising lactose. Lac operon is inducible.
Q108 | Chemical Control and Coordination Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following pairs of hormones are not antagonistic (having opposite effects) to each other? (1) Parathormone – Calcitonin (2) Insulin – Glucagon (3) Aldosterone – Atrial Natriuretic Factor (4) Relaxin – Inhibin
(1) Parathormone – Calcitonin
(2) Insulin – Glucagon
(3) Aldosterone – Atrial Natriuretic Factor
(4) Relaxin – Inhibin ✓ Correct
Solution: Relaxin is a hormone which is secreted by the placenta, it relaxes the ligaments of the pelvis to ease parturition. While inhibin is secreted from the anterior pituitary, it decreases the secretion of FSH. So they are not antagonistic.
Q109 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:0.90
Microtubules are the constituents of (1) Cilia, Flagella and Peroxisomes (2) Spindle fibres, Centrioles and Cilia (3) Centrioles, Spindle fibres and Chromatin (4) Centrosome, Nucleosome and Centrioles
(1) Cilia, Flagella and Peroxisomes
(2) Spindle fibres, Centrioles and Cilia ✓ Correct
(3) Centrioles, Spindle fibres and Chromatin
(4) Centrosome, Nucleosome and Centrioles
Solution: Microtubules are made up of tubulin proteins. Spindle fibres, centrioles and cilia have a considerable percentage of microtubules within them.
Q110 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:0.90
A complex of ribosomes attached to a single strand of RNA is known as (1) Polysome (2) Polymer (3) Polypeptide (4) Okazaki fragment
(1) Polysome ✓ Correct
(2) Polymer
(3) Polypeptide
(4) Okazaki fragment
Solution: Polysomes are formed when two or more ribosomes are attached to a complex of mRNA molecules.
Q111 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:0.90
Fertilization in humans is practically feasible only if (1) the sperms are transported into vagina just after the release of ovum in fallopian tube (2) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the fallopian tube (3) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the cervix (4) the sperms are transported into cervix within 48 hrs of release of ovum in uterus
(1) the sperms are transported into vagina just after the release of ovum in fallopian tube
(2) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the fallopian tube ✓ Correct
(3) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the cervix
(4) the sperms are transported into cervix within 48 hrs of release of ovum in uterus
Solution: The process of fusion of a sperm with an ovum is called fertilization and it occurs at the ampullary – isthmic junction of the fallopian tube if sperm and ovum are transported simultaneously to this region.
Q112 | Breathing and Exchange of Gases Easy
ok conf:0.90
Asthma may be attributed to (1) bacterial infection of the lungs (2) allergic reaction of the mast cells in the lungs (3) inflammation of the trachea (4) accumulation of fluid in the lungs
(1) bacterial infection of the lungs
(2) allergic reaction of the mast cells in the lungs ✓ Correct
(3) inflammation of the trachea
(4) accumulation of fluid in the lungs
Solution: In asthma inflammation of bronchi and bronchioles occurs. It is a chronic problem so allergic reactions in the respiratory tract are more frequent which may lead to allergic reactions of the mast cells in the lungs.
Q113 | Plant Growth and Development Easy
ok conf:0.90
The Avena curvature is used for bioassay of (1) ABA (2) GA3 (3) IAA (4) Ethylene
(1) ABA
(2) GA3
(3) IAA ✓ Correct
(4) Ethylene
Solution: Bioassay is a quantitative method used to correlate the curvature obtained in a plant after the application of an external stimulus like light and the amount of growth substance produced endogenously.
Q114 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:0.90
The standard petal of a papilionaceous corolla is also called (1) Carina (2) Pappus (3) Vexillum (4) Corona
(1) Carina
(2) Pappus
(3) Vexillum ✓ Correct
(4) Corona
Solution: The papilionaceous corolla are uneven in size and one of them is largest called vexillum.
Q115 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:0.90
Tricarpellary, syncarpous, gynoecium is found in flowers of (1) Liliaceae (2) Solanaceae (3) Fabaceae (4) Poaceae
(1) Liliaceae ✓ Correct
(2) Solanaceae
(3) Fabaceae
(4) Poaceae
Solution: Family Liliaceae comprises monocot plants and monocots have trimerous flowers i.e., floral parts are multiple of three so is the carpel.
Q116 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:0.90
One of the major components of cell wall of most fungi is (1) Chitin (2) Peptidoglycan (3) Cellulose (4) Hemicellulose
(1) Chitin ✓ Correct
(2) Peptidoglycan
(3) Cellulose
(4) Hemicellulose
Solution: Fungal cell wall is made up of chitin, a complex polysaccharide which contains nitrogen. It is made of a linear polymer of N-acetyl-D-glucosamine monomers.
Q117 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:0.90
Select the incorrect statement (1) FSH stimulates the sertoli cells which help in spermiogenesis (2) LH triggers ovulation in ovary (3) LH and FSH decrease gradually during the follicular phase (4) LH triggers secretion of androgens from the Leydig cells
(1) FSH stimulates the sertoli cells which help in spermiogenesis
(2) LH triggers ovulation in ovary
(3) LH and FSH decrease gradually during the follicular phase ✓ Correct
(4) LH triggers secretion of androgens from the Leydig cells
Solution: The reproductive cycle in the female primates is called menstrual cycle and it is repeated at the interval of 28 or 29 days. During Proliferative phase, also called follicular phase, of menstrual cycle, the endometrial lining starts regenerating and, in the ovary, primary follicle grows and matures into Graafian follicle simultaneously, due to rise in gonadotropins (LH and FSH). So, LH and FSH *increase* during the follicular phase, not decrease.
Q118 | Cell Cycle and Cell Division Easy
ok conf:0.90
In meiosis crossing over is initiated at (1) Pachytene (2) Leptotene (3) Zygotene (4) Diplotene
(1) Pachytene ✓ Correct
(2) Leptotene
(3) Zygotene
(4) Diplotene
Solution: Crossing over is a major event in meiosis cell division that results in variations. It occurs in the pachytene sub phase of meiosis-1.
Q119 | Principles of Inheritance Easy
ok conf:0.90
A tall true breeding garden pea plant is crossed with a dwarf true breeding garden pea plant. When the F1 plants were selfed the resulting genotypes were in the ratio of (1) 1:2:1:: Tall homozygous : Tall heterozygous: Dwarf (2) 1 : 2 : 1 :: Tall heterozygous : Tall homozygous : Dwarf (3) 3:1:: Tall : Dwarf (4) 3:1:: Dwarf : Tall
(1) 1:2:1:: Tall homozygous : Tall heterozygous: Dwarf ✓ Correct
(2) 1 : 2 : 1 :: Tall heterozygous : Tall homozygous : Dwarf
(3) 3:1:: Tall : Dwarf
(4) 3:1:: Dwarf : Tall
Solution: When selfing between F1 plants is done, gametes are formed. It was observed that a gamete carries either a recessive allele or a dominant allele but not both the alleles at the same time. Due to this reason, the law of segregation is known as the law of purity of gametes.
Q120 | Biodiversity and Conservation Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following is the most important cause of animals and plants being driven to extinction? (1) Over exploitation (3) Habitat loss and fragmentation (2) Alien species invasion (4) Co-extinctions
(1) Over exploitation
(2) Alien species invasion
(3) Habitat loss and fragmentation ✓ Correct
(4) Co-extinctions
Solution: The conversion of forests into agricultural land and building of dams or roads in a natural habitat results in its fragmentation; this has caused extinction of animals and plants.
Q121 | Ecosystem Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following is a characteristic feature of the cropland ecosystem? (1) Absence of soil organisms (3) Absence of weeds (2) Least genetic diversity (4) Ecological succession
(1) Absence of soil organisms
(2) Least genetic diversity ✓ Correct
(3) Absence of weeds
(4) Ecological succession
Solution: The Cropland ecosystem is a man-made ecosystem, and the cultivated crops belong to one or two plant species at a time. Hence, genetic diversity is least in such ecosystems.
Q122 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:0.90
Changes in GnRH pulse frequency in females is controlled by circulating levels of (1) estrogen and progesterone (3) progesterone only (2) estrogen and inhibin (4) progesterone and inhibin
(1) estrogen and progesterone ✓ Correct
(2) estrogen and inhibin
(3) progesterone only
(4) progesterone and inhibin
Solution: The reproductive system is controlled by GnRH secretion from the brain which is under the influence of the amount of estrogen and progesterone is circulated. GnRH pulse frequency increases from one pulse every 90-100 min in the beginning of the follicular phase which increases in the late follicular phase.
Q123 | Biotechnology-Principles and Processes Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following is not a feature of the plasmids? (1) Independent replication (3) Transferable (2) Circular structure (4) Single - stranded
(1) Independent replication
(2) Circular structure
(3) Transferable
(4) Single - stranded ✓ Correct
Solution: Plasmids are found in bacterial cells independent of the control of chromosomal DNA. It is a single circular strand of DNA (double-stranded, not single-stranded). The statement is "Single-stranded" so it is incorrect.
Q124 | Structural Organisation in Animals Easy
ok conf:0.90
Which of the following features is not present in Periplaneta americana? (1) Schizocoelom as body cavity (2) Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development (3) Exoskeleton composed of N-acetylglucosamine (4) Metamerically segmented body
(1) Schizocoelom as body cavity
(2) Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development ✓ Correct
(3) Exoskeleton composed of N-acetylglucosamine
(4) Metamerically segmented body
Solution: Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development is a feature of vertebrates and certain invertebrates like echinoderms.
Q125 | Human Health and Diseases Easy
ok conf:0.90
In higher vertebrates, the immune system can distinguish self-cells and non-self. If this property is lost due to genetic abnormality and it attacks self-cells, then it leads to (1) Allergic response (3) Auto-immune disease (2) Graft rejection (4) Active immunity
(1) Allergic response
(2) Graft rejection
(3) Auto-immune disease ✓ Correct
(4) Active immunity
Solution: In auto-immune disease the immune system mistakes the body cells as foreign and starts attacking them.
Q126 | Principles of Inheritance Medium
ok conf:0.90
Match the terms in Column-I with their description in Column-II and choose the correct option.
